title: Chilean Sea Bass - Chilean Lumber Drug Trafficking Operation

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Chilean Sea Bass - Chilean Lumber Drug Trafficking Operation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Subject: Freedom of Information Act Request – Chilean Lumber Drug Trafficking Operation

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request records maintained by the Office of the Director of National Intelligence concerning the June 2026 seizure by Chilean authorities of approximately 100 tons of cocaine, ketamine, and other illicit narcotics concealed within lumber or timber shipments.

Specifically, I request:

1. Intelligence reports, Intelligence Information Reports (IIRs), finished intelligence products, assessments, situation reports, intelligence notes, analytical products, briefing materials, executive summaries, threat assessments, or similar records generated, received, reviewed, disseminated, or maintained by ODNI concerning the seizure, the associated trafficking organizations, transportation routes, source countries, destination countries, or related criminal networks.

2. Records reflecting intelligence sharing, coordination, information exchange, operational support, analytical support, or joint investigative efforts between the United States and the governments of Chile, Bolivia, European Union member states, Europol, Interpol, or any foreign law enforcement or intelligence service concerning the seizure or related trafficking activity.

3. Records identifying, discussing, assessing, or analyzing whether the narcotics associated with the seizure were intended for distribution within the United States or whether any U.S.-based criminal organizations, transnational criminal organizations, cartels, facilitators, brokers, importers, exporters, financial networks, or other entities were linked to the shipment.

4. Intelligence assessments, strategic intelligence products, technical reports, analytical memoranda, threat assessments, or briefing materials created from January 1, 2024, through the date of the search discussing the concealment of narcotics within lumber, timber, wood products, chemically impregnated wood products, or similar concealment methodologies.

5. Records provided to, received from, transmitted to, referenced by, relied upon, summarized by, disseminated to, or coordinated with the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), Department of Homeland Security (DHS), U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP), DEA Special Operations Division (SOD), Department of State, Department of the Treasury, Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA), National Security Agency (NSA), National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC), or any other federal agency, Intelligence Community element, task force, fusion center, foreign government, international organization, or law enforcement entity concerning this matter.

6. Records sufficient to identify ODNI intelligence products, reports, assessments, dissemination records, briefing documents, or analytical products concerning the seizure, including title, date, originating office, classification level, recipient office, and distribution information.

Referral and Consultation Request

To the extent that responsive records originated with, were received from, contain equities of, or are otherwise subject to review by another agency, component, office, or government entity, I request that such records be processed pursuant to applicable FOIA consultation and referral procedures.

Please do not deny or administratively close portions of this request solely because another agency originated, contributed to, or maintains an interest in the responsive records. Instead, I request that responsive records be referred or consulted as appropriate for release consideration.

For each referral or consultation made, please provide the name of the receiving agency or component, the date the referral or consultation was initiated, any assigned tracking number, and a description sufficient to identify the records referred where such disclosure would not reveal exempt information.

Requested Action

• Conduct a search reasonably calculated to locate responsive records.

• Release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions.

• Provide responsive records electronically.

• Provide a Vaughn index describing records withheld in whole or in part.

• Describe the foreseeable harm supporting any withholding pursuant to the FOIA Improvement Act of 2016.

Fee Waiver Request

The requested records concern a significant international narcotics trafficking investigation and intelligence activities relating to transnational organized crime. Disclosure is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations and activities. This request is made for news-gathering, research, and public-interest purposes and is not for commercial use.

Related News:
Chile seizes wood laced with 'historic' 100 tons of drugs
Dharvi Vaid with AFP
06/09/2026June 9, 2026
Chile has announced its largest-ever drug seizure. About 1,080 tons of wood were found laced with illegal drugs — mostly cocaine and ketamine — headed to Europe.
https://www.dw.com/en/snorting-sawdust-chile-seizes-wood-laced-with-historic-100-tons-of-drugs/a-77467556


Respectfully,

William Fernandes

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

William Fernandes

title: Records concerning Xavier Harmon (former Computer Technology teacher) – suspension, hearing, termination, Discord use, cryptocurrency, and record removal

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Records concerning Xavier Harmon (former Computer Technology teacher) – suspension, hearing, termination, Discord use, cryptocurrency, and record removal
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

Public records in the possession, custody, or control of the Steel Center for Career and Technical Education relating to Xavier Harmon, former Computer Technology teacher, and matters leading to his suspension, disciplinary charges, hearing(s), and termination of employment.

This request is made in the public interest and is based in part on records that were previously made public by Steel Center and appear to no longer be publicly available online.

Records Requested:
1.  School Board, Hearing, and Disciplinary Records-  Records related to any hearing, proceeding, disciplinary action, suspension, or review involving Mr. Harmon, including hearing notes, transcripts, audio/video recordings, exhibits, board packets, draft and final minutes, administrative summaries, memoranda, recommendations, and records of deliberations. This includes records that were previously available online but are no longer publicly accessible (including archived or draft copies).
2.  Discord and Electronic Communication Platforms-  Records relating to Mr. Harmon’s use of Discord or similar platforms with students, including server names, administrative usernames, policies on platform use, and investigative materials.
3.  Communications with Students - Records reflecting communications between Mr. Harmon and students from August 1, 2018 through February 28, 2022 (emails, messaging platforms, logs, complaints, and investigative summaries). Student educational records protected by FERPA may be redacted.
4.  Complaints and Investigations-  Records of allegations, complaints, investigations, findings, or referrals concerning:
•  Intimidation or discouragement of students
•  Improper contact with students or families
•  Violations of directives prohibiting contact after February 16, 2022
•  Referrals to law enforcement or outside agencies
5.  Cryptocurrency- Related Conduct Records relating to allegations or investigations of cryptocurrency mining/trading using school equipment, student involvement, unauthorized software, or financial benefit.
6.  Records Management - Records retention schedules for these matters, plus documentation of the removal, deletion, or unpublishing of public records related to Mr. Harmon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Citizen Commission

title: Preschool Development Grant data

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Preschool Development Grant data
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Databases or information related to the Preschool Development Grant, including the Infant-Toddler Startup/Expansion Subgrant in Arizona. Specifically, for each grant recipient, I request the following information at the grant-to-provider level, as available:

1) Provider name 
2) Provider license ID
3) Employer identification number (EIN) if nonprofit provider
4) Employer identification number (EIN) if for-profit provider
5) Street number
6) Street name
7) City
8) Zip code
9) Grant application date (separately for each grant round, if applicable)
10) Grant application submitted date (separately for each grant round)
11) Grant awarded date (separately for each grant round)
12) Grant payment date, if differs from the awarded date (separately for each grant round)
13) Grant award amount (separately for each grant round)
14) Indicator for meets poverty criteria, e.g., low-income community or high percent of students eligible for Free and Reduced lunch (separately for each grant round)
15) Indicator for meets Opportunity Zone criteria (separately for each grant round)
16) Indicator for meets rural/tribal census tract criteria (separately for each grant round)
17) Indicator for meets performance data criteria, e.g., ELA or Early Childhood Assessment (separately for each grant round)
18) Indicator for meets 25% preschool gap criteria (separately for each grant round)
19) Indicator for meets capacity to serve infants/toddlers criteria (separately for each grant round)
20) Grant application score (separately for each grant round)
21) Any other variables that factor into the grant scoring criteria or the calculation that determines the awarded amount (separately for each grant round)
22) All of the above items 1-21 for rejected grant applications 

If available, I also am requesting information on the process of applying for the grants, information on the process of allocating the grants including the grant formula, and information on any other grants available to providers from the state.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gabrielle Pepin

title: Freedom of Information Act Request: Regulatory Examinations of City Lending, Inc. (2020-2023)

communications.subject: DC Freedom of Information Act Request: Freedom of Information Act Request: Regulatory Examinations of City Lending, Inc. (2020-2023)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To the DISB FOIA Officer:

Under the District of Columbia Freedom of Information Act (D.C. Official Code § 2-531 et seq.), I am formally requesting the complete, unredacted production of the following administrative and investigative records:

1. The full administrative file, examiner notes, internal communications, and formal findings regarding the regulatory examination of City Lending, Inc. conducted by the DISB Banking Bureau (specifically involving Brian Williams and/or Philip Edmonds) in or around March 2020.
2. The full administrative file, examiner notes, internal communications, and formal findings regarding the subsequent regulatory examination of City Lending, Inc. conducted by the DISB Banking Bureau in or around January and February 2021.

This request is submitted to complete the formal administrative record regarding the agency's continuous oversight of this regulated entity. These files must be produced to be reviewed in conjunction with the active, ongoing FOIA demands regarding the third investigation of City Lending, Inc. conducted by Mark Pendleton and authorized by Sharon Shipp in 2023.

To preserve the evidentiary integrity of these files, all requested records—including internal emails, examiner notes, and administrative logs—must be delivered in their native metadata format (e.g., .PST, .MSG, .EML, or native Microsoft Office files). Do not flatten these files into PDFs, as doing so destroys the original authoring, routing, and timestamp data required for oversight review.

Because this request involves a matter of significant public interest regarding the efficacy of municipal regulatory oversight and the administrative shielding of predatory lending practices, I request a complete waiver of all associated search and duplication fees. The production of these records should be provided free of charge as it primarily benefits the general public and legislative oversight bodies.

I expect a determination regarding this request within the statutory 15-day window.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

title: LONGVIEW SCHOOL DISTRICT – 2026 Sean Turpin 42 U.S.C. § 1983 civil rights lawsuit

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: LONGVIEW SCHOOL DISTRICT – 2026 Sean Turpin 42 U.S.C. § 1983 civil rights lawsuit
communications.communication:
Via email: publicrecordsrequest@longviewschools.org

PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST [RCW 42.56]

TO: Public Records Officer, Longview School District

DATE: June 9, 2026

Dear Public Records Officer,

Pursuant to the Washington State Public Records Act (RCW 42.56), I am requesting copies of the following identifiable public records and writings that were created, received, maintained, or relied upon in the care, custody, or control of the Longview School District between January 1, 2026, and February 15, 2026.

RE: Official Meta (Facebook) and X (Twitter) Administrative Action Logs

All native administrative activity logs, audit trails, account history logs, and moderation event data for the District’s official Facebook page and X account generated between January 1, 2026, and February 15, 2026. This request explicitly requires the production of native records or platform exports showing:

1.	The exact timestamps, IP addresses, and user profiles of any District employee, agent, or contractor who toggled, disabled, or restricted the "comment" functionality on any post published by the District.
2.	The native platform log records demonstrating the hiding, deleting, or filtering of comments associated with user profile "Sean Turpin" or email address "seanrturpin@gmail.com".

DEFINITION OF RECORDS AND WRITINGS

RECORDS AND WRITINGS as defined by RCW 42.56.010(4): any handwriting, typewriting, printing, photostating, photographing, and every other means of recording any form of communication or representation including, but not limited to, letters, words, pictures, sounds, or symbols, or combination thereof, and all papers, maps, magnetic or paper tapes, photographic films and prints, motion picture, film and video recordings, magnetic or punched cards, discs, drums, diskettes, sound recordings, and other documents including existing data compilations from which information may be obtained or translated.

PRODUCTION PROCESSING AND DELIVERY OF ELECTRONIC RECORDS

Please provide all records in their native electronic format. Please do not instead create new records by printing electronic files then scanning and printing the newly scanned prints.

To preserve metadata and ease review, please produce as follows:

Emails: PST ( Microsoft Outlook ) or MBOX containers; if unavailable, EML/MSG with full headers.
Chats/Texts/IM: native exports where supported; otherwise, CSV/JSON with fields for sender, recipients, timestamps (UTC), message/thread IDs, and attachments (export attachments in original formats).
Calendars: ICS where possible; or PDF plus any attachments.
Documents/attachments: original/native formats (DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, PDF, etc.).
Logs/Reports: CSV/JSON preferred.
Delivery: Direct electronic transmission via email attachments or open (no login/registration/password) download link hosted by your agency (e.g., SharePoint/OneDrive public link). Please do not use third-party portals that require creating an account or logging in. A single or series of .zip files is fine.

If any records cannot be produced in the above formats, please produce them in their native format with associated metadata intact.

METADATA REQUESTED

Where reasonably available, please include customary metadata fields such as: From, To, CC, BCC, Subject, Sent/Received Date-Time (with timezone/UTC), Message-ID, Conversation/Thread-ID, file names, file sizes, hash values (if held), and folder/location paths.

SEARCH DETAILS AND RECORDS PRESERVATION

Please identify the systems searched e.g.  (Exchange/Outlook/Office 365, Teams, mobile devices, MDM archives, JustFOIA/CivicPlus admin, helpdesk, file shares) and the custodians.

Please describe the search methods/terms used.

If any potentially responsive sources were not searched or are no longer available, please explain why and what preservation steps were taken.

USE OF THIRD PARTY PORTALS NOT MANDATORY

Please note that under Washington’s Public Records Act, use of a third-party “portal” is not mandatory for requestors. Agencies may not condition access to records on the creation of an account, the use of a login, or the acceptance of third-party vendor terms.

Third-party portals (such as GovQA, NextRequest, JustFOIA, or similar systems) often:

Require account creation or login credentials;

Restrict downloads, viewing times, or re-access;

Remove or disable links after a limited period;

Place records behind commercial platforms operated by for-profit corporations.

These restrictions unlawfully burden and condition access to public records. The Public Records Act forbids agencies from imposing access barriers beyond those authorized by statute.

RCW 42.56.080(2): An agency “shall not deny a request for identifiable public records solely on the basis that the request is overbroad.” Agencies must provide the “fullest assistance” and “most timely possible action” to requestors.

RCW 42.56.550(1): Courts are directed to liberally construe the PRA to ensure access and strictly construe exemptions against withholding.

Fisher Broadcasting-Seattle TV LLC v. City of Seattle, 180 Wn.2d 515, 528 (2014): Agencies must produce records in a “reasonably usable format when feasible.”

Mechling v. City of Monroe, 152 Wn. App. 830, 841 (2009): PRA is to be liberally construed in favor of disclosure and cannot be restricted by agency practices.

Because of this, I respectfully decline use of any third-party portal. Please provide all responsive records via:

Direct electronic email attachment to this requestor’s address, or

A direct, open (no password, no registration required) cloud-based download link hosted by your agency.

Do not register this requestor with, nor direct this requestor to, any third-party commercial portal, including but not limited to FOIAOnline, GovQA (a Granicus Corporation), NextRequest, FOIAExpress, JustFOIA, FOIADirect, or any similar online service.

Do not transmit any portion of this request, or responsive records, to any for-profit vendor or portal. Doing so would unlawfully condition access to public records and contravene both RCW 42.56.080 and RCW 42.56.550.

PUBLIC RECORDS REQUESTS SUBMITTED VIA EMAIL

This public records request is being submitted via email, consistent with Washington’s Public Records Act.

RCW 42.56.080(2) provides in relevant part:

“Agencies shall honor requests received in person during an agency’s normal office hours, or by mail or email, for identifiable public records unless exempted by provisions of this chapter. No official format is required for making a records request; however, agencies may recommend that requestors submit requests using an agency-provided form or web page.”

Accordingly:

A request submitted by email is a valid, enforceable method of making a PRA request.

Agencies may recommend, but cannot require, the use of an online portal or official form.

Any refusal to honor this request as submitted would directly conflict with RCW 42.56.080 and the agency’s duty under RCW 42.56.100 to provide the “fullest assistance to requestors” and adopt reasonable rules that do not “unreasonably hinder or delay” access.

Therefore, this request is to be honored and fulfilled by your agency via email, as submitted. Should the agency fail to process this request in compliance with statute, relief will be sought pursuant to RCW 42.56.550 in the form of judicial review, penalties, and attorney’s fees.

INSTALLMENTS AND FEE ESTIMATES

Please provide:

An initial acknowledgment within five business days (RCW 42.56.520) with a reasonable time estimate for each installment, and

A cost estimate if fees are anticipated (please itemize and explain).

NISSEN AFFIDAVIT AND ALL DEVICE SEARCH REQUIRED

Pursuant to Nissen v. Pierce County, 183 Wn.2d 863 (2015), and its progeny, this request specifically includes all responsive public records contained on personal devices or personal accounts (including personal cell phones, text/SMS/MMS, personal email accounts, cloud storage, instant messaging apps, and social media accounts) of any agency employee, official, or contractor where public business may have been conducted.

To comply with this requirement, please:

Conduct a comprehensive all-device search, including both agency-issued and personally owned devices/accounts used for public business.

Require each potentially responsive custodian to execute a Nissen affidavit or sworn certification affirming:

That they conducted a diligent search of all personal and agency devices/accounts reasonably likely to contain responsive records;

The methods used to conduct the search (e.g., search terms, accounts checked, device types); and

Whether any responsive records were located, preserved, and produced.

Produce all responsive records located in such searches along with the executed affidavits.

Failure to obtain and provide sworn Nissen affidavits or equivalent certifications from custodians may constitute an incomplete search in violation of RCW 42.56.520 (requirement for a reasonable and adequate search) and may expose the agency to relief under RCW 42.56.550.

EXEMPTION LOGS

Please ensure that all redactions or exemptions claimed by your agency in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log.

The log must:

* Identify the valid legal reason (statutory citation) for each exemption;
* Specify the location of each redaction within the record produced (via footnote, coded reference, or margin notation);
* State the number of pages or records withheld in their entirety, if any; and
* Demonstrate that only the **minimal exempt portion** of any record has been withheld, as required by law.

The Washington Public Records Act requires agencies to justify each withholding or redaction:

RCW 42.56.210(3): “Agency responses refusing, in whole or in part, inspection of any public record shall include a statement of the specific exemption authorizing the withholding of the record (or part) and a brief explanation of how the exemption applies to the record withheld.”
RCW 42.56.210(1): Exemptions must be narrowly construed.

Washington courts have repeatedly enforced this duty:

Rental Housing Ass’n of Puget Sound v. City of Des Moines, 165 Wn.2d 525, 538 (2009): Agencies must provide specific explanations for each exemption; conclusory or vague justifications are insufficient.
Sanders v. State*, 169 Wn.2d 827, 845 (2010): “The burden of proof is on the agency to establish that refusal to permit public inspection and copying is in accordance with a statute.”
Resident Action Council v. Seattle Housing Auth.* 177 Wn.2d 417, 433 (2013): Agencies must disclose the non-exempt portions*of records and cannot withhold more than necessary.

Therefore, I request that your agency provide an itemized exemption log with each installment of records produced, in compliance with RCW 42.56.210(3) and the above case law, ensuring transparency and accountability in the redaction process.

COMMERCIAL PURPOSE DECLARATION

Commercial Purpose Declaration

This public records request is not being made for any commercial purpose. The Washington State Public Records Act (PRA) prohibits agencies from disclosing “lists of individuals” for commercial purposes.

RCW 42.56.070(8) states:

“This chapter shall not be construed as giving authority to any agency to give, sell, or provide access to lists of individuals requested for commercial purposes….”

Accordingly:

This request does not seek a “list of individuals.”

This request is not made for any commercial purpose.

This declaration satisfies all requirements of RCW 42.56.070(8) regarding prohibitions on lists and commercial purposes.

Warning: The PRA does not authorize agencies to require requestors to complete additional “commercial purpose declarations” when the request does not seek a list of individuals. Such requirements improperly condition access to public records in violation of RCW 42.56.080(2) (requests must be honored by mail or email, no official format required) and RCW 42.56.100 (agencies must provide the fullest assistance to requestors and may not adopt rules that unreasonably hinder or delay access).

Washington courts have consistently rejected agency attempts to impose conditions not found in statute:

Mechling v. City of Monroe, 152 Wn. App. 830, 841 (2009): The PRA is a strongly worded mandate for broad disclosure; agencies cannot erect unauthorized barriers.

SEIU 925 v. University of Washington, 193 Wn.2d 860, 875 (2019): The PRA is to be liberally construed to promote access, and exemptions must be narrowly applied.

Therefore, no additional commercial purpose declaration will be completed for this request. Any attempt to impose such a requirement would be an unlawful restriction of rights under RCW 42.56.

PRESERVATION OF PUBLIC RECORDS *** ANTICIPATE LITIGATION ***

WAC 44-14-03005 RETENTION OF PUBLIC RECORDS

WAC 44-14-03005 (Retention of Public Records) makes clear that public records are subject to retention schedules, and that unlawful destruction can constitute a crime.

RCW 40.16.010 and RCW 40.16.020 criminalize the unlawful mutilation, destruction, or concealment of public records.

RCW 42.56.100 expressly prohibits an agency from destroying any public record after a public records request has been made for that record, even if the record would otherwise be scheduled for lawful destruction.

In addition, agencies have heightened retention obligations when litigation is reasonably anticipated. Records potentially relevant to actual or anticipated litigation must be preserved until both the public records request and the litigation have been fully resolved.

Legal Consequences of Premature Destruction

In Yacobellis v. City of Bellingham, 55 Wn. App. 706, 780 P.2d 272 (1989), the Court of Appeals held that an agency violates the Public Records Act and is subject to attorneys’ fees and statutory penalties when it destroys responsive records after receiving a public records request.

By contrast, it is not a PRA violation if a record was destroyed prior to the agency’s receipt of a request, provided the destruction was lawful under retention schedules. See Bldg. Indus. Ass’n of Wash. v. McCarthy, 152 Wn. App. 720, 218 P.3d 196 (2009); West v. Dep’t of Natural Resources, 163 Wn. App. 238, 258 P.3d 78 (2011).

Washington courts have emphasized that the Public Records Act (RCW 42.56) and the state’s records retention laws (RCW 40.14) are separate but complementary. Agencies must comply with both.

Accordingly:

Your agency must preserve all potentially responsive records immediately upon receipt of this request.

No responsive records may be altered, deleted, or destroyed until this request has been fully resolved and any related litigation has been lifted.

Any unlawful destruction of responsive records may expose the agency and its officials to criminal liability (RCW 40.16), civil penalties, and mandatory attorneys’ fees (RCW 42.56.550(4)).

RECORDS INSTALLMENTS - ORDER OF PRODUCTION

If production of responsive records will be completed in multiple installments, production in reverse chronological order is strongly preferred to the extent supported by native system exports, provided it does not delay the delivery of installments.

DIGITAL COMPRESSION AND FILE MINIMIZATION REQUESTED IN ACCORDANCE WITH RCW 42.56

Pursuant to RCW 42.56.080(2), please provide all responsive electronic records in the fewest number of individual files, preserving native formats wherever possible. Where appropriate, please use free digital compression (.zip or equivalent) to consolidate responsive records into the smallest number of installments for efficiency and economy.

This approach reduces agency burden, promotes prompt disclosure, and minimizes potential costs to the requestor, consistent with the Public Records Act’s directive that agencies provide records in the most “reasonably locatable form” and “fullest assistance to requestors.” See RCW 42.56.100 (agency duty to adopt reasonable rules for providing fullest assistance); RCW 42.56.120(2)(b) (fees must reflect actual costs, not used to discourage access).

Washington courts have consistently held that agencies must facilitate access in a practical, non-burdensome manner. See Fisher Broadcasting-Seattle TV LLC v. City of Seattle, 180 Wn.2d 515, 528 (2014) (agencies must provide records in a usable format when reasonably feasible); Mechling v. City of Monroe, 152 Wn. App. 830, 841 (2009) (PRA is to be liberally construed in favor of disclosure). Producing consolidated, digitally compressed files satisfies these obligations by reducing unnecessary fragmentation and excess per-page charges.

Accordingly, I request that each installment be produced as a single compressed .zip file (or as few as technically necessary), containing all responsive records in native format, to ensure both compliance with RCW 42.56 and an economical calculation of any applicable public records fees.

COMMUNICATION VIA EMAIL

Please send all requests for clarification, status updates, and production of responsive electronic documents via direct email reply.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. We look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Good Luck.

Subject: Appeal of RTKL Denial – Request #430020 (Logan W. Cumberland Incident, July 2, 2024)
Date: June 10, 2026
To: Butler County District Attorney’s Office

Attn: Open Records Appeals Officer
124 West Diamond Street
P.O. Box 1208
Butler, PA 16003-1208

Dear Appeals Officer,

I am formally appealing the denial issued by Cranberry Township on June 9, 2026, regarding my Pennsylvania Right-to-Know Law request #430020.

Original Request Summary:

I requested all records related to the July 2, 2024 incident involving Logan W. Cumberland in Cranberry Township, PA — including initial response reports, dispatch logs, statements, scene photos/video (non-bodycam), and communications with PSP/FBI. 

Grounds for Appeal:

The Township has not met its burden of proof under the RTKL. A blanket citation to §708(b)(16) without a detailed explanation or index of records is insufficient. The agency must specifically demonstrate that every withheld record falls entirely within the exemption.

Pursuant to §706 of the RTKL, the agency is required to segregate and release any non-exempt portions of the records. This includes potentially releasable items such as:  
- Basic administrative or logistical information (e.g., dispatch timestamps, resource usage summaries, overtime costs) 
- Any final summary, closure notice, or non-investigative elements that do not reveal the institution, progress, or result of the criminal investigation.

This was a high-profile officer-involved shooting that resulted in a shelter-in-place order and significant public concern. Releasing any available non-exempt information serves the public interest while still protecting legitimate investigative concerns.

I respectfully request that you conduct a full review of the responsive records (including in camera review if necessary), order the release of any segregable non-exempt portions, and issue a detailed Final Determination explaining any continued withholding. Thank you for your prompt attention to this appeal. I look forward to your determination.

Sincerely,
Citizen Commission

title: Obama Presidential Library: White House Records Concerning Groupon CEO Andrew Mason

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: White House Records Concerning Groupon CEO Andrew Mason
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to contacts between President Barack Obama (or his administration) and Andrew Mason, who served as CEO of Groupon from the company's founding through his resignation on February 28, 2013.

Scope of Request
I request all responsive records dated between January 20, 2009 and February 28, 2013, including but not limited to:

Reports, memoranda, and briefing materials
Emails and text messages
Paper correspondence
Call logs referencing Andrew Mason
Visitor logs and access records

Specific System Queries
I specifically request that the following system be queried:

White House Worker and Visitor Entry System (WAVES): Please search for all entries associated with Andrew Mason during the above date range.
Presidential Information Network (PIN): Please search for all entries associated with Andrew Mason during the above date range. The PIN system logged internal White House scheduling, contacts, and communications, and may contain records of calls, meetings, or correspondence involving Mason that would not appear in WAVES.

Background
Andrew Mason was the founding CEO of Groupon, a prominent technology and e-commerce company headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. Federal Election Commission records reflect that Mason contributed $5,000 to Obama for America (Obama's 2012 re-election campaign) and $30,800 to the Democratic National Committee. This request seeks to understand the nature and extent of any official contacts between Mason and the Obama White House during his tenure as Groupon CEO.

If certain parts of this request or if certain portions must be redacted, please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. In plain English: please weigh on the side of showing, line-by-line, exactly what each exemption is.

Thank you for the work that the Obama Presidential Library does and for the commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Obama Presidential Library: White House Photographs - February 1, 2011 - Hosni Mubarak speech, call and statement

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: White House Photographs - February 1, 2011 - Hosni Mubarak speech, call and statement
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request under the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) for all still photographs taken by White House photographers on February 1, 2011.

Background:
February 1, 2011 was a significant day at the White House due to rapidly developing events in Egypt. In his memoir, Obama speechwriter and adviser Ben Rhodes described the day in detail, including: an unscheduled appearance by President Obama in the White House Situation Room, where he and foreign policy principals watched a televised address by Egyptian President Hosni Mubarak; a subsequent call placed by President Obama to President Mubarak from the Oval Office, with senior officials listening on speakerphone; and evening televised remarks by President Obama on the situation in Egypt (transcript and video available at the White House Archives).

Link to transcript:
https://obamawhitehouse.archives.gov/the-press-office/2011/02/01/remarks-president-situation-egypt/

Scope of Request:
I request all still photographs taken by any White House photographer — including but not limited to Chief Official White House Photographer Pete Souza and any other staff or contract photographers working that day — on February 1, 2011. This request specifically includes, but is not limited to, photographs taken:

In the White House Situation Room
In the Oval Office (including during and around the call with President Mubarak)
Before, during, and after President Obama's televised evening remarks

This request is limited to still photographs only and does not include WHTV video footage or other motion picture recordings.

title: Body-Worn Camera Footage and Records – Jan 22, 2026, Officer-Involved Shooting (Jaque Rabon) (San Bernardino County Sheriff)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Dear Public Information Officer,

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 7920.000 et seq.), and specifically expanded transparency mandates under Penal Code Section 832.7 (Senate Bill 1421) and Government Code Section 7923.625 (Assembly Bill 748), I am requesting public records related to the lethal use of force encounter on January 22, 2026, at approximately 10:00 A.M. at 3782 Tyler Street, Riverside, CA, involving decedent Jaque Rabon.

I request the following specific records:

- Video and Audio Recordings: All body-worn camera (BWC) footage, dashboard camera footage, and audio recordings capturing the encounter, radio communications before, during, and after the incident, as well as the immediate events leading up to and following the discharge of lethal force.

- 911 and Dispatch Logs: All radio transmissions, computer-aided dispatch (CAD) logs, and audio recordings of dispatch communications regarding this incident.

- Investigative Reports: All initial incident reports, supplemental reports, deputy narratives, use-of-force reports, supervisory reviews, and other records generated, maintained, or possessed by the your agency relating to this incident.

Names and Badge Numbers: The identification of all deputies involved. This includes first, middle, and last names, ranks, badge numbers, and assignments, to the extent disclosure is required under Penal Code Section 832.7.

Critical Incident Package: Any critical incident briefing materials, press releases, public summaries, scene diagrams, photographs, and evidentiary materials prepared.

Per Government Code Section 7923.625, the statutory 45-day window for public disclosure of critical incident video has elapsed. If the Department asserts that disclosure would substantially interfere with an active investigation, please provide the factual basis supporting that determination, identify each withheld record, state the specific statutory exemption relied upon, and provide the date on which the Department expects the interference to cease and the records to be released. If portions of any responsive records are exempt from disclosure, please redact only the exempt portions and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. Pursuant to Government Code Sections 7922.535 and 7922.540, please provide a determination regarding this request within the statutory timeframe.
Please provide all responsive materials in native electronic format to avoid duplication fees. If fees are required, please notify me in advance if the total exceeds $20.

Thank you,

Inland Empire Legal Literacy Project

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jaz Williams

title: Contract Request: Interfolio

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Contract Request: Interfolio
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

The most recent contract, governing, base, or master license agreement with Elsevier relating to the licensing of Interfolio;
All related terms and conditions, terms of service, amendments, addenda, appendices, attachments, product terms, MOUs, MOAs, data processing agreements, and/or letters of intent; 
All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to the contractual relations with Elsevier for faculty information system technology;
and
Assessment documentation including, but not limited to, the Higher Education Community Vendor Assessment Toolkit (HECVAT). 

Responsive materials include all contracts, agreements, amendments, appendices, invoices, and any other documents listed above that incorporate language governing terms of use of Interfolio.

The scope of this request should encompass more than just direct contracts or renewals between the university and Elsevier; please incorporate all applicable agreements and supporting records facilitated through consortium entities (such as Lyrasis).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

mo

communications.communication:
​Dear mo,

This email is to acknowledge receipt of your Public Record Request RR-02539. I will begin gathering responsive records and submit them to you in accordance with applicable Federal and State Law and University Policy when they become available.

CLICK HERE to contact me with questions related to this request or to send additional attachments related to this request that you may have forgotten to include with your initial submission. communications.subject: Re: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Contract Request: Interfolio
communications.communication:
Dear Mo,

Regarding your Freedom of Information Act request below, we anticipate that
we will reply to your request electronically. However, pursuant to Section
3 (1) of the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, a request from a person
"must include the requesting person's complete name, address, and contact
information," and an address "must be written in compliance with United
States Postal Service addressing standards." Contact information "must
include a valid telephone number or electronic mail address."

Please provide the missing information required by the statute, and we will
be happy to process your request.

Thank you,

Matthew Riggs
FOIA Compliance Specialist Associate

Suite 3300 Ruthven | 1109 Geddes Ave. | Ann Arbor, MI 48109-1079
